# `energy/state_distribution_billing_efficiency_pct`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: Distribution billing efficiency (%, by state)  
**One-line**: Share of energy actually billed to a consumer, out of total energy input to the distribution system.  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_distribution_billing_efficiency_pct.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_distribution_billing_efficiency_pct.json)

## Definition

Share of energy actually billed to a consumer, out of total energy input to the distribution system. Billing efficiency = (energy billed) ÷ (energy input). The complement of billing-side losses (theft, unmetered consumption, under-billing). 100% = every kWh that enters the grid was billed to someone.

- **Spatial**: India (states + UTs) (36 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 522

## Methodology vintage

NITI Aayog ICED ``/energy/electricity/distribution/operationalPerformanceStates``. Category ``billing-efficiency``.

## Notes

Together with collection efficiency, billing efficiency decomposes the commercial half of AT&C losses: AT&C loss ≈ 1 − (billing × collection / 100).
